steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage defines horizontal gene transfer (HGT), notes it is common among prokaryotes, and builds to a practical conclusion: understanding how to prevent HGT 'might result in the mitigation of dangerous pathogens.' The question asks what the passage primarily emphasizes.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — study of HGT may yield useful applications: matches the passage's practical conclusion — understanding HGT could help mitigate dangerous pathogens. ✓
  • Choice B — HGT is more prevalent than earlier views suggested: the passage makes no comparison to earlier views of HGT's prevalence. ✗
  • Choice C — HGT presents new research findings: the passage does not frame its content as presenting new or novel research findings. ✗
  • Choice D — HGT among prokaryotes is better understood than among eukaryotes: the passage draws no such comparative claim. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
